frames: The agent stands upright, then begins to tilt forward and slightly to its right.
It continues to lean further to the right, with its lower segment approaching a diagonal angle.
The entire body is now significantly angled, with the upper segment also tilting rightward.
The agent loses balance, with its lower segment nearly horizontal and the upper segment sharply angled.
The right side of the lower segment lifts off the ground as the agent begins to rotate counter-clockwise.
The agent is mid-rotation, with its upper segment pointing downwards and the lower segment angled upwards.
The rotation continues smoothly, bringing the upper segment almost parallel to the ground.
The agent completes a full flip, landing on its lower segment in a reversed orientation.
